description: Slow emotional love ballad with piano and strings, building from intimate whisper to powerful declaration
suno_tags: pop ballad, emotional piano, string orchestra, female vocals, slow tempo, heartfelt, 72 BPM
vocal_style: Warm and intimate in verses, building to powerful belted delivery in choruses. Slight vibrato, emotionally raw, capable of both whispered vulnerability and soaring high notes.

tips: Start verses with quiet, intimate imagery — a touch, a glance, a memory
Chorus should be a direct emotional declaration — say what the heart feels
Use sensory details: warmth of skin, sound of breath, taste of tears
Bridge should reveal the deepest vulnerability or a turning point
Keep chorus under 4 lines for maximum singalong impact

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Grand Piano]

[Verse 1]
[Soft]
Your fingerprints are on my soul
Traced in lines I can't erase
Every silence tells the story
Of the world inside your face

[Pre-Chorus]
And I've been holding back this flood
But darling, here it comes

[Chorus]
[Belted]
I would cross a thousand midnights
Just to hear you say my name
Every heartbeat writes your letters
I will never be the same

[Verse 2]
The coffee's cold, the morning's grey
But golden where you lay your head
I memorize the way you breathe
Like prayers I've left unsaid

[Pre-Chorus]
And all the walls I built so high
You walked right through the door

[Chorus]
[Belted]
I would cross a thousand midnights
Just to hear you say my name
Every heartbeat writes your letters
I will never be the same

[Bridge]
[Whispered]
Maybe love was always this
A slow unraveling
Of everything I thought I was
Becoming everything

[Chorus]
[Powerful]
I would cross a thousand midnights
Just to hear you say my name
Every heartbeat writes your letters
I will never be the same

[Outro]
[Soft]
I will never be the same
[Grand Piano]
[End]
do: Build dynamics from whisper to full power across the song
Use piano as the anchor instrument throughout
Write from genuine emotional experience — specificity creates universality
Let the bridge be the emotional peak before the final chorus
Use strings to swell during chorus sections
dont: Don't use cliches like 'baby' or 'babe' without purpose
Don't make every line rhyme — let some breathe naturally
Don't rush the tempo — trust the slow build
Don't overload verses with more than 4-6 lines
Don't describe love abstractly — show it through specific moments
